method: Same base model in both conditions. Control = no skill; Treatment = solana-brain loaded (read SKILL/RESOLVER/orchestration/clusters; Set B also given seeded .solana-brain memory). Answers uncapped. Blind judge scored identity-neutral files; Set A on 5 dimensions, Set B on objective continuity vs known prior decisions.

setA_quality_under_pressure.verdict: Parity. An already-expert base model has little headroom on one-shot answers; the skill matches it (and ties on gate-discipline). The skill's value is not raw knowledge.

setB_memory_continuity.verdict: Categorical win. Without solana-brain the model cannot recall the project's prior decisions and gives generic advice; with it, the model recalls and honors Anchor / OtterSec audit-before-mainnet / the Squads 3-of-5 / Helius / Cayman+DE and refuses to contradict a settled decision.

honest_caveats: Single LLM judge; small N (4 + 2 tasks).
Same elite base model both sides, so Set A has little headroom by design.
Set B is a capability test: a plain LLM has no per-project memory — that gap is the point of the feature, not a trick.
Set A position: Assistant A = control, B = treatment (fixed); identity hidden from the judge, but slots not alternated.
